title: Evidence-Gated Shorts Factory
summary: Twenty logical channel slots share public-safe evidence contracts while private identities, media, source payloads, consent receipts, and operating results stay in the private runtime.

sections.heading: Approval model
paragraphs: A versioned evidence gate is the approval authority. When all bound criteria pass, the gate emits a SHA-256 receipt and releases only the declared execution plan into an open loop. There is no discretionary editorial human approval gate.
bullets: Claims require independent, primary, fresh evidence plus contradiction review and uncertainty disclosure.
Originality, asset rights, synthetic-content disclosure, privacy, policy, quota, lease, and hash integrity are mandatory.
Any changed or expired input invalidates the receipt and closes the loop.
YouTube-required user consent is an action-specific receipt consumed by the gate, not the gate's approval authority.

sections.heading: Loop separation
bullets: Closed loop selects topics, accepts claims, evaluates evidence, and decides continue, revise, stop, or scale.
Evidence approval releases private upload, processing polling, scheduled publication, and private metrics refresh as bounded open-loop steps.
Trend metadata is a hypothesis input only and cannot approve claims or publication by itself.
Every superseding decision retains the old receipt and evidence links.

sections.heading: Repository boundaries
bullets: myhome-jarvis owns public-safe orchestration and evidence-gate contracts.
myhome-external-evidence-lake owns provenance-rich public trend metadata envelopes.
dsl-raid owns executable architecture and document-code traceability semantics.
myhome-shorts-factory owns private channel bindings, content jobs, consent and gate receipts, OAuth handles, media, and outcomes.

sections.heading: Verification
sections.code:
go run ./cmd/shortsctl docs check
go run ./cmd/shortsctl verify
go run ./cmd/shortsctl gate evaluate fixtures/shorts_gate_pass.json
go test ./internal/shortsfactory ./internal/repogovernance
